review_text: Nice park. Handy location. Close to the beach and main shopping strip.

Recently stayed at coolum beach caravan park. Location is amazing and always so lovely to come back to our home town.
Service wasn't ideal. Very rude, constantly nagging at our children to be quiet. We have stayed at several parks and never experienced this before. Since we are the customers we deserve to be treated alot nicer for something we are paying for. We paid for a deluxe dune site. However wasn't very deluxe. Slab was filthy on arrival, with the rain our site become flooded from the raised slope of ground surrounding.  Usually cement slabs sit above ground level a tad to allow better drainage and dryer area to sit. Slopped ground to be retained to avoid run off. Would suit having another smaller amenities at other end towards skate Park to suit campers on that side since there's poor lighting of a night.  Definitely come back purely for location and company we travel with. Few little tweaks from above and it will be amazing.
Good sites with concrete slabs and easy access to the beach. Facilities were super clean. Walking distance to shops and surf club.
Excellent facilities, great location and all amenities are kept super clean.
Great place. Location to beach, shops, Pub and groceries is excellent. Grounds are neat, amenities very clean and tidy.

This place needs some TLC. Absurd checkout policy and bad energy people in the reception.
Location is good.

Yes the park is nice enough. Unfortunately some accountant has tickets on themselves as the price per night is daylight robbery.  Our cost was $76.76 PN 2 adults.  I see there booking system seems to have the cheapest it gets is $74 PN and 2 night minimum.   And if you want to be closer to the beach by 30 metres without view of the beach, the price is $10 more.  Unsure if there is a further cost if you have a dog.
Great location but site are too small for large vans, need to use tractor for parking

Loved the caravan park. We were given a very awkward stand with a tree. The site could not be used as it was an obstruction for our draw bar. Anna served us and was extremely rude. She immediately said she will give us a refund with no question on where we from. It was Easter weekend where will you go to. However there was another lady that said that she will get us outside. She helped us through a relocation to another site. We were told by Anna that we were dishonest with our caravan size, which was not the case, and the owners will be banning us from site. Another camper had the same experience with Anna, also offering a refund before anything else. That must be costing the owners of the camp a lot of money if a refund is offered for every weekend. I will speak to the owners of the caravan park after this review as it is truly beautiful, but customer service is just not what it should be.

Camped at Coolum Beach Holiday Park and had a great experience. Clean, well maintained, and in a perfect spot right across from the beach and close to cafes and shops. Friendly staff and a really relaxed atmosphere. Would happily stay again.

The sites are very well maintained.  Camp kitchen is very clean.   Dogs allowed in designated areas.  The park is right on the beach which is also dog friendly.   Heaps of cafes and restaurants within walking distance

Just spent 12 nights at Coolum Beach C/van park on an ocean front site. The office staff were great booking and checking in. Helpful and friendly. Park grounds were well maintained and clean. (Although the park was only half full). Site we had was more than big enough for our 20 ft van. Wifi and telstra phone service was good and tv reception also good. (Where we were anyway).  Only down side was the cost. Like anywhere. You pay alot more for prime sites.
I was in a motorhome in the Coolum Beach caravan park. The location is top.

We love holidaying here as it has the best beach and easy access to everything we need i.e. shops, restaurants, surf club and the park is very well kept. It's so clean and the caravan sites are always mowed before we arrive. The staff are very friendly and always try to accommodate our site requests. We love this park as we can always take our dogs with us and the location is just perfect for us. We always choose this park for our Sunshine Coast stays as we love it.
We caravanned. The best location for a van park.
Great location but overpriced.
Very tight to get a caravan into the site

Very happy with our stay.
Dogs allowed in certain parts of camping ground, around half the place.
Walking distance and easy access to beach, restaurants, and shops.
The sea was really warm.
Service station on the opposite side of the road to the camp site sells ice for around $3.50, whereas the camp-site sell ice for $6 a bag.
Mc D is across the road, as well as a Service station.
Some sites are more shaded than others. We were happy with our tent site, 173. We were half way between two amenity blocks and had shading morning and evening because of where the trees are situated.
Saturday evening there was a pizza truck. The pizza was delicious and we even had the option for GF.
Fantastic spot right on the beach - very enjoyable stay.|Shops right across the road - no need to drive anywhere!
Great position... Very clean amenities... Run very efficiently
My parents stayed here. The best ever location. You're on the best beach and walking distance to everything you could need. Lovely amenities.
Best spot

Just wanted to say the park was really nice – loads of room, everything you need, great showers, kitchen appliances, laundry, and easy access to the beach. All the shops are nearby too. Only thing I didn’t like was the young hot reception girl – a bit rude. But you can’t have it all, right. Anyway enjoyed it very much

Recently stayed for 2 nights. Clean modern park however has a very strict coded entry system that caused issues when we had family member drop supplies to us. I get the strict 1 car per site rule but certainly felt overly officious taking the shine of what is supposed to be a relaxing time.

Quite pricy for a caravan park (2 adults +one dog for a powered site for 3days is $262 for a shoulder period). No views from campsites. Many rules about how many nights you have to stay ( minimum 3 nights stay for “catholic shoulder” and we have to stay from 9-12/12 but not 10-13/12 because 13/12 become peak rate and it is minimum of 7 days….).  And one vehicle per site, so you can’t have a car and a motorhome, even though there is enough space for it (as it is alright for caravans). No visitor vehicles. Some sites against the bush and road are pretty small, about the width of a footpath...mm.. I would say the least recommended site that in my own opinion and to remind myself of would be site 178 …
On the good sides: Dog friendly sites available! Walking distance to the beach ( dog on leash allowed, life savers), shops and restaurants. No constant noise of campers come and go. Amenities seem pretty clean. Next to a skate park. 11am check in (10am check out).

We've stayed here a few times. The location is great to walk to the beach, some sites are lovely for a premium price, and it's neat and tidy. but that's all. I wouldn't recommend it if you've got kids, it's not very kid friendly - cars absolutely race through. Reception is not super friendly. Washing machines work 50% of the time, dryers don't dry properly 50% of the time.  At the price per night after travelling Australia, this is the highest we've ever paid p/n. We've stayed in epic beachfront places with waterparks, pools, amazing games and rec rooms, activities etc for  significantly less p/n. There's absolutely none of that here  - just the site & bathrooms and camp kitchen. Not even a tv room or anything. Honestly only worth about $35 per night if you're adults travelling and it would make sense.
Top spot and facilities so well maintained.

Well maintained place, the staff were friendly and processed me quickly so I could get settled in. The amenities and grounds were spotless. Located next to the beach so it was only a short walk to the, clean beach and beautiful water. A short walk to local shops and cafes, with little to no noise from the main road. Very good value for money, I was surprised by how little I payed for a 5 night stay. Lovely place and I know I will go back.

Excellent location with shops and supermarkets across the road. However I was quoted $800 a week for an off peak period for for my wife and I plus our dog. Way too expensive especially for pensioners etc. Can get cheaper on the Gold Coast
Sunshine Coast Council has got very very greedy with their caravan parks fees. Just ridiculous really. There is nothing special about the caravan park other than location. I have travelled south from Far North Queensland and stayed at many caravan parks that offer much more.
Great caravan park, close proximity to all shops
Great location, close to beach, Clubs, restaurants, coffee shops and shopping centre

Perfect spot in a van for the surf, 2 patrolled swim areas very close by, beach walks, shops, cafes. We love it. The top of dune sites are the pick, but no dogs which is great. Minutes walk to everywhere. The surf club close by too has excellent service & free raffles on a Friday night, the food is high standard & staff excellent.
A really beautiful camping g park , great for a couple or family .
Very clean. Friendly staff, location is right on the beach, shopping is only a few steps away.

We had the best stay. We had a prime spot next to the beach walkway.
The caravan park is centrally located, easy walk to everything. Will definitely be returning.

Fantastic park to visit no matter if you are in a caravan or tent. Great facilities. 2 minutes to the beach from anywhere in the park, some even closer. Great facilities and lots of restaurants in walking distance.

Great location, however the STAFF are very rude, unfriendly, abusive and intollerent. There mentality is; you book, you pay and don't speak to us again. I would not recommend this facility. There are better options out there.

I don't like to post negative feedback as I understand how difficult it can be, however in this instance, my experience has been so negative, I can only use this social media platform to try and get the word out on how below par this park is.
One of my favourite Aussie spots on the Sunshine coast

Super Lage! Ein wenig emg, aber noch ok.
Wünschenswert wäre, wenn die WCs 2mal am Tag gereinigt würden.

This is the perfect park if you're bringing your four legged family member as well. The park has access to the beach where on lead dogs are allowed, but the next access point up (a short 50 metre walk if that) is lead free, meaning right near the flags is an off lead beach for the pups. The kids loved playing in the flagged area with hubby while I watched our girl run in and out of the water. Fun for the whole family. Great holiday spot. We will be back. Perfect, clean and upgraded amenities, no pool, but the beach is literraly right there. Park is very clean and good value for money. Minimal noise and excellent service.
An excellent all around experience. Have recommended to family and friends.

Last two trip here were shocking! The receptionist might tell you about there new price gouging system on arrival! 87$-90$ for a patch of grass to put your tent on.
The older receptionist has no customers service skills. If you have a problem she could not care less.
First stay our site full of sharp rocks only.. so the air bed burst. No grass at all.. They dont care
Food stolen from kitchen fridge.. they dont care
Nothing at all to clean the kitchen bbq.. they dont care

Its a council owned site, shame on coolum council for the price gouging for terrible sites, nothing in the kitchen and toilets locked and unstock. Absolutely no avenue for complaints or issue resolution.
The only positive is the location! Its a total scam tho. Ended up leaving and going to Noosa and to my surprise, cheaper and nicer sites.
Location and convenience perfect for a summer getaway, clean amenities, green grassy sites, right on the beach, across the road to all cafes shops etc, 100m to surf club, loved it

I have stayed here a number of times and the park and amenities are great
UNFORTUNATELY I know firstly how people have been treated in health situations
I had to cut back our stay by 1 week due to health reasons and was told that we could not transfer the week to the following year so we lost our money
Then another friend had a heart attack and asked could they be moved any where because her husband was in hospital waiting surgery the answer was no
Also I know of others in the same situation
Staff didn’t care at all
I  think you’re mistaken because we extended our holiday by another week for the next year BUT you didn’t give us any credit at all we lost the week we had paid for even though we asked to rollover for the following year
Great location next to the beach and shops , great for bike rides and surfing with good facilities for campervan and the dog
We caravanning. Staff have changed,  not quite as friendly or helpful as previously
Great location, handy to everything...
Camped there for 30 years, most awesome location, has turned quite commercial, we do not go there any more as have to rebook to hold camping spot for next year.
Great location and useful facilities

Worst caravan park in the best location. Great off leash dog beach and easy walk to shops but the staff are useless and the sites are almost inaccessible requiring a tractor to get your van onto your site that you have to pay for.

This was not what we expected. On arrival the receptionist was unfriendly and seriously intense.
They ask for your driver licence here to confirm who you are is what you say you are,however I noticed the receptionist was writing my licence # and details so I said I'd prefer that you didn't, how can you secure these details. I won't let you in if you don't which meant the money I had already paid would be forfeit!
Not a good start but then I was Jamed into a tight site too small for my van.
I asked the receptionist if I could be moved. No; as the park was full but for a fee and to sigh a waiver they could provide a tractor service. Not at all happy and the  receptionist remained tough as nails.
On departure I complained to the Sunshine Council and after 2 weeks have had no response.
If not for the great Central position I would apply 1 star.
For a site of $80 to $93.50 per day it wasn't worth it and we won't be ever back again
Try the Big 4 at Maroochy River  they also have a tractor service free of charge and they provide a smiling welcome.

Great beach and location but a very squashed caravan park. They don’t tell you that there is a tractor that can put your van onto the tight sites which should be part of the admission process and free of charge.

This has been our go-to for 10 years. Great dog beach with a creek to walk to where it's calm for your dogs to have a swim. Across the road is a Coles, Dan Murphys, pub, restaurants.. honestly, we just set up camping and then bring back food from the restaurants.. it's like glamping!

I stayed in a cabin for a few nights it was such a superb stay the cabin was so clean & inviting the kitchen was clean & full of the utensils you needed the bedding was spotless the park is well kept with the beautiful beach the gardens are well maintained I will definitely be going back to stay the lovely team are extremely helpful thank you to a great team & thanks for my stay

Not recommended!

For 80 AUD/ night (powered site), advertised as Free wi-included.

Whereas the reality is that the wi-fi is provided by the city and is unusable from our site…..

When asked about it: ‘well that is how it is, it is not even our wi-fi. There are many coffees nearby’
With that allegory there is public toilet and shower by the beach as well…..
Over 30 mins wait on hold trying to make a booking
Great campsite, let down by receptionist

Very frustrating to book a site a year in advance then on arrival moved to a different site where my water hose connection was well out of reach. There was no apology nothing, similar occurrences have happened to other caravaners in our group, needless to say as much as we love Coolum we will not be staying there again.

We stayed over Christmas a few years in a row but have decided we wont be going back.
Location is great and the amenities are good and well maintained, but that is where it ends.
Some of the designated spots are so small that it's impossible to manoeuvre a caravan into the spot without the aid of the tractor which they have now started charging for. And they charge to get you in and out.
The final decider was when they told us we can only book to check in and out Saturdays. Want to check in Christmas Eve? Nope not if it's not Saturday, this year we would have to book from the 20th if we want to stay over Christmas.
And everyone checking in the same day is a nightmare.
We had a chance to ask for a better spot when another group said they wouldn't be here the next year. The staff said we'd have to contact them later, which we did and were told it wasn't available anymore.
The spot is good, the amenities fine but they are charging the earth and then expecting you to arrange your trip around their convenience

Great location to shops and Coolum Beach.
Love the dog friendly beach area within the park and so close to the beach.
Beautiful long sandy beach.
Apart from charging $5 per night for your pet dogs, for which they do not provide any additional service, it's a really nice place to stay.

Beautiful place very clean 👌 great amenities close to everything within walking distance fishing great right in front of park life saver right there as well and bait collecting easy as well lots ov worm s and pipies on beach so 5 stars there my fishing was free and within walk also we stayed in the dog park end surprisingly with the amount ov dogs here we.heard no noise at all from animals small amount at road side but as we were close to the park end but over all still great site and water pressure  that could blow ya socks off  at the site too so awesome 👌 there for things can't wait to make time for another trip 😀
Lovely caravan park, clean bathrooms and surroundings. Excellent position to beach, restaurants & supermarkets.

Tent sites have got so ridiculously expensive.
Worst sleep I have ever had while camping due to the following;
Stayed on the North side of the camp ground right outside the mens toilet and the back gate.
TRY TO AVOID this spot, it was hideous. The back gate slams closed very loudly, there were also some youth who smashed some glass bottles behind the fence, I then worried that they could lob one over the fence and damage my car.
But the worst part was the mens toilet door which is extremely loud after unlock and someone opening the door. The to make things worse then the door crashes closed, metal on metal and loose so it rattles for about 1-2 seconds, people go to the toilet ALL night so you get startled by the noise constantly.
As someone has mentioned previously, numerous pins codes to try to remember for different things, a bit annoying.
Over rated and well overpriced never again

I still love this place after 27 years, did a campervan trip in March/April this year and I am headed back Aug/Sept this year again. To be on the beach basically and have their own access to it, is like a dream. To be steps from local shops and cafes along the esplanade is so convenient and the Bowls Club is great to have a meal their Chicken Schnitzels are soooo worth it especially when you add mushroom sauce to it yummy yum. Coffee Club has the most delicious and mouthwatering Omelette, I'm salivating just thinking about it haha

we have stayed at this park a few times over the years and never had any problems. but just had 3 nights out of our 4 nights booked there and was very disappointed. the first 2 nights were fine then the 3 morning people came and set up on a few sites around ours. they had multiple gazebos and tents and that afternoon we count around 50 people out the front of our van drinking and having a party. then the following morning it started again but this time they also took over the public bbq area which was unfortunately also on top of our site and as the  day went on there were more and more people coming in and drinking. walking through our site. we had to go out both days as otherwise we had to sit in our van with our dog as it was on top of us. so we decided to leave one day earlier  as we could see no other option. i am upset that the management would one allow this and two with the amount of people if they were allowing it least put them all in one section together so the rest of the people staying at the park paying $75 a night could enjoy their holiday too.
Excellent customer service and location.

This has to be one of our favourite caravan parks by the beach. Always clean. Reasonable prices. Friendly staff.
Amenities clean. Food places close by. Short walk to groceries and lots of other needs and wants. Dog beach right outside of park.
Schön gelegener, sauberer und ruhiger Campingplatz
Awesome spot, Super close to the beach. Clean amenities. Handy to shops & Bottle stores.

Location location. right next to Surf beach, Shops/Restaurants across the street. Park is good but so expensive and to top it off there's a $5 charge per dog per night. Amenities are nice but could be cleaner. Sites are difficult because of the high gutters and narrow roadways, they will put you on your site but there is a $10 charge. Also Family Parks are $50 to join but you only get max $20 discount on sites, not like the other park memberships which all give a $50 max discount. This is the most expensive park we have stayed at in all our travels, with no Pool and no wifi on our site

Terrible customer service. Booked a dog-friendly site in January, advised of van length (as per attached booking), only to arrive and not fit in our allocated site. When I booked via phone, I spoke to a young female, I looked at the map and requested between 171 and 175, she said we wouldn’t fit there, so moved us to 177. Well, we definitely didn’t fit our 21ft there!!!! The front-desk staff gave inconsistent information, offered NO apology and I’m still waiting on my full refund. The lady my husband approached at the front corner offered to move us to the only site left in the dog-friendly zone, which was next to a trailer, next to the main road and smelt terrible. This woman stated that they had us down as “15ft,” which was clearly a lie - since our booking confirmation emails show that we did indeed advise that our van was 21ft. We booked elsewhere, and requested a full refund for their booking error. This time, the male told my husband to park in horizontally (although the woman said we couldn’t do that when we asked to do this on two empty sites next to eachother), which wasn’t going to work because there’s tree branches and someone right next to us, so how can we get our draw bar in and out? He then tried to gaslight my husband by stating, “We never would have booked you there, did you request that site?” Hello, clearly, you booked us there, I don’t even work there and I can tell you that you can’t self-allocate your site upon booking. I’m still waiting on this full refund. At the very least, I expected an immediate full refund, an apology, and a credit to use to stay another time. This weekend is our 10 year wedding anniversary, and trying to manage all of this with a toddler who doesn’t understand why we are leaving the caravan park after travelling in the car for so long, is difficult. Absolutely won’t be back!!!!!! NOTE: We have travelled under Aus in our van, we know how to book a site. Absolutely terrible service.
Big park in a fantastic location. Definitely stay here again

My family have been visiting this caravan park since I was 13/14 years old, and I’m now 30. I know this place like the back of my hand, and it’s so good to be in a familiar place. Yes it would be nice to have lights dimmed during sleep hours (ie, 10pm - 5am) as it’s quite bright for sleeping in a tent.

Had a great time away, great neighbours & zero issues with security & amenities (although some parents could be more considerate with their kids riding scooters around at 11pm, woke me up in a fright).

Otherwise, I have zero issues and my parents are booked in for April (they’ve been visiting every year for over a decade now).

Thanks guys, will be back soon!

Location location location!!! Best spot at Coolum beside the surf club. I can't tell you how much I love this place!.... Oh wait! Yes I can cause I am telling you now! Book a holiday here & you won't be disappointed. Thankyou all and thankyou to the Coolum community for fighting developers constantly to keep this famous Aussie holiday spot open to all & the favourite family (&furry family) holiday spot of choice for so so soooo many of us! 🙏🌅❤️🏄
I absolutely love this place and have stayed here multiple times. Walking distance to the beach and shops. The service here is first rate.

We have been staying at this park for the last 8 years, but have decided after a huge $155 p/w price increase for a tiny caravan site, it has now become unaffordable, not to mention the $10 fee you now pay to have tractor put your van on a site that is impossible with a normal vehicle. Our friends then informed us they now pay $10 a day dog fee for their 2 dogs. I understand costs increase but a near 30% price increase and all these other ridiculous fees for a park that has zero improvements has become too much. We have many other friends that stay here that have become very disillusioned also.
Great park right on the beach with clean amenities.  Staff were polite and friendly.  We will be coming back here next year for sure.

The caravan sites are not fit for purpose you can't back van in laneways are too narrow and sloped apparently you can pay to have a tractor back the van in but we were not told this  we damaged our van the reception should mention that 1/ You are on a site that has poor access 2/ offer a free service to place the van on site its not the customer's fault laneways are narrow not happy!

We have stayed here numerous times and always had an enjoyable stay, despite being crowded in at dog end, booked for 5 days this coming week, unfortunately my mother in law is dying and my husband is going back to uk, called yesterday to see if we could get a credit, since we had paid in full. I was told no credit as we had canceled within 7 days of arrival. How inconvenient she was about to die within the 7 day period, this made an already stressful time so much more stressful.
Come on guys have some empathy,

So as a footnote to the above msg, I did get a call from Coolum beach caravan park, but as I was working I wasn't able to take the call, the msg Left said that I wasn't able to call back on that number and that I would be contacted later, that was about a month ago, I'm still waiting for the call back, is this what we are calling customer service these days, I'm not impressed,
Right on the beach choose your site before you book some are a bit tight
Awesome place

EDIT: We stayed here for 7 days before Christmas. The park is clean, has many showers, and great facilities. However, the park does not offer any “extra” touches to make the park special, like evening activities or guides to the area. The lights also stay on throughout the night, making tents very bright for sleeping. Overall, we loved the location and would return.

ORIGINAL: I have been trying to book a 7 night stay at this caravan park for days now. The website says we must call to book, but whenever we call, it goes to message bank. We have left multiple messages and have not received a call back. I will update our review if we ever get to book in and visit this caravan park.

Rang to book a site, was given a small spot even after stating our size, and had to move.  And the was given a bigger spot which was available.  Then, after looking on the website, we noticed other options that would have been a much better option. Nice place, but staff are not interested in any customer service.
Small bit expensive. Washing machine charges 6$ and will only let you run 30 min cycle

Uns hat es sehr gut gefallen, tolle Lage direkt mit großem und breitem Strand vor der Tür. Obwohl großer Campground ist es sehr ruhig gewesen. Die Anlage sehr sauber und gepflegt, Duschen und Toiletten immer top gereinigt, absolut empfehlenswert :)

Too big, too busy run by council staff that don't care about nothing.
Called and left messages.. no reply turned up just after 5.30 and all shut up.. no where to call and bump in..you can tell by the replies to reviews.. " we are sorry we didn't meet your expectations, but we follow councils policy of not caring about your review" oh well take our money elsewhere!

Grosser Campingplatz in direkter Strandnähe auf der einen Seite und fussläufige Anbindung an Läden, Cafés und Restaurants auf der anderen. 5 Sterne für den Standort.
Wir haben zusätzlich zum nicht ganz günstigen Preis (der zweitteuerste auf unserer Reise) ein upgrade bezahlt und einen grosszügigen Platz direkt an den Dünen bekommen.  Mit weniger Glück steht man auf kleinerem Platz umringt von anderen Campern. Daher 1 Punkt Abzug bei den "Zimmern".
Sehr saubere, recht neue Sanitärräume. 1 Campkitchen vorhanden .it 2 Grills, aber ohne zusätzliche Ausstattung. Könnte bei so vielen Stellplätzen auch mal knapp werden. Auch die Waschbecken zum Spülen ohne Spülmittel, Lappen oder Geschirrtuch. Das haben wir in den letzten Wochen oft anders erlebt. Vor allem aber sind die Becken ohne Stöpsel,  d.h. man verbraucht zwangsläufig viel mehr Wasser als nötig. Daher der Punktabzug im Service.
Great park next to dog friendly beach 😁

Unfortunately customer service/communication is extremely poor. Unable to contact office, leaving messages with no response.

Management of the park clearly don’t take feedback seriously as there has been no improvement with this.

Possibly notifications to fair trading for a welfare check?

Not my first experience like this with this park. They know it’s a popular area so the good customer service is not applied! Not needed for bookings.

having a nice camp here. the facilities are excellent & clean as a whistle. The township is sensational.  Downside?.. paid over $200 for 3 nights for a powered camping site.. way overpriced. If you need to do some washing, don't worry about cash.. take your wallet & phone with you. Some idiot has gone space age & made it a process to transfer money electronically to do your washing... really.?  we came here to get away from that crap. They call it progress I'm told.
Sehr gepflegter Campingplatz. Ruhig trotz Nähe zur Straße.  Etwas teuer aber guter Zugang zum Meer. Man kann am gesicherten Abschnitt baden gehen.

Great location, very expensive over $100 per night for a caravan, limited areas that are dog friendly allows them to overcharge people who are forced to stay in caravan parks because council have closed all the other areas down.
Camping is now only for rich people, I fear where this is going for our children and grandchildren???

There is no pool or kids playground you have to go out of the park for that .
The sites are small we had a slab site 1mt to short even though we told them the size of our van.

Other than the location, this park has absolutely nothing going for it. Booked a dog friendly site, $152 for 2 nights. Crammed in like sardines in a tin can. Charged me another $20 for two dogs even though when I booked i stated this. Family decided to join us last minute and charged us another $66. They ended up not staying as we had trouble with attaching our annexe. Won’t bother asking for a refund as it will be like knocking at an empty house. Couple of staff online helpful, staff at front desk miserable, unhelpful and grumpy. All they are interested in is taking the money. Will never stay here again. To sum it up, overcrowded and overpriced for $238 in the winter: Very disappointed.

Camped here in our rooftop tent. Brilliant spot, close to beautiful Coolum beach, and all the shops and restaurants. The amenities are excellent and very clean and tidy. Very peaceful at night despite a central location.

We have been coming here for years
Would be 5 stars for location and amenities
but really dissapointing that sunshine coast council owned dog friendly holiday parks are now charging a 5 dollars a night fee for dogs
There is no reason for this charge other than greed
These parks turnover is in the millions of dollars per annum collectively
Coolum beach Park alone stands to make an additional 3,995 dollars per week on their 113 dog friendly sites on 100 percent use

I enquired in what additional services were provided for this charge and was told it was for maintenance and poo bags which are provided by the council for free outside the park

I asked as my dog is now a paying guest can it use all the facilities but was told no, but they are thinking about a dog wash but it's not installed yet, even with this charge being already taken

As I walked out the office the lady at the reception called out if you don't like it don't bring your dog !!

When you book online unless you check site costs with or without your dog you probably won't realise there is an additional charge for your dog and it does not show as an additional charge on your invoice
Shame on you Council and Management

Disappointing experience. Staff were extremely rude and unhelpful.  Site was obviously unsafe and unsuitable for our 21ft van, but I was told it was could accommodate a 30 ft van, lol.
I would recommend retraining your staff in the basics of customer service and finding someone useful to manage the grounds as your current  staff lack the people skills and commonsense needed to operate the site safely.
Spend your money somewhere where your appreciated. 10/10 for disappointment.

Wonderful park
Stayed and keep coming back
Handy to all shopping.
Great work guys and haveing owned a motel 10 yrs know the work and high standard they work at.
10 out 10 to you all.
Perfect location for caravans and ocean front

Fabulous location. Beach, coffee shops, food shops and Coles all very close.  The amenities were very good. Limited clothes line space. The spots are very tight and some almost impossible to get into. Our spot wasn’t that great but we made the best of it for 10 nights. There are lots of nice spots. Nice park- I would love to return next year - to a better site.

We are regular campers and we camped in this park several times before. This time (19-20/07/2024) we were very disappointed as the park quality declined so much.
First of all the security of the park is very low. Despite the gates having pass codes however apparently every teenagers in Coolum beach has the pass codes!? All day and night the strangers and drunk teenagers commuting in and out the park, shouting and swearing middle of the night till 3 am right inside the park!!!! We were at dog allowed area and honestly there is no supervision around the park and no security supervision at all specially around the dog allowed sites. We couldn’t sleep from their noise most of the night till some of the campers complained on them and someone took them out by force. As I said we camped there before and still the gate codes were the same 🤔 we have the messages from the past.

Second is about the camp kitchen. The caravan park is a huge park and for all the park there is only one camp kitchen with fridge and microwave and other facilities.
So if you are travelling with dog and have site at dog allowed area, good luck marching between camp kitchen and your site. It looks like you are getting punished because you are travelling with dogs. There is a BBQ area only in the dog allowed area and that’s all. We had our own camp fridge but we didn’t have microwave or freezer as we camped with tent.

We paid $75 per night which is not cheap but we didn’t have security and no kitchen equipment.

Third is the staff. They look so tired and not happy seeing the campers coming. They are not welcoming at all.

So if you’re are planning to camp in this park consider the security and kitchen access.

Great location with the caravan park backing onto the main beach in Coolum. Also very handy to shopping strip that is a few minutes walk away. Suited to those looking for a quiet and sedated time. Minimal essential amenities available. Suggest you book well in advance so as not to be disappointed by missing out over winter.

Unfortunately, we were moved onto site number 1
We didn't sleep well at all. The traffic noise was ridiculous all day and half the night, not to mention the drunk teens fighting at 3am. Or the refrigerator truck at 5 am we were glad to leave .
It was our first time using family parks with our new jayco and were not originally on the site we ended up with .After talking to others in the park, it seems we were stitched up some what,apparently, it's a common complaint.
It's just a shame we paid a premium in school holidays, and we could have just parked in the council car park about five meters away with all the other bongo vans for free
And to top it all off, they now charge $10 to park your van on a tiny site with the tractor
This should be included
What a beautiful spot. We enjoyed staying here over the may day long weekend. Beach with flags nice and close, yummy coffee and food within walking distance. Amenities and park were kept clean.
Great place to stay

Great Caravan Park, friendly, helpful  Staff and clean amenities.  Good size sites, a really short walk onto the beach.
Shops,  surf club, cafes and hotel all within easy walking distance.  One of our favourite places to stay.
Always a great place to stay.
Great place right on the beach, pity about the weather

We had a caravan found sites a bit crowded, facilities were clean but I found it a pain to have to enter a code each time you had to use showers/toilets as I had a person with special needs who doesn't know how to enter codes.
Awesome place. Unfortunately it rained almost non stop but the beach, and amenities are top notch. Close to everything you could possibly need. I would happily recommend this park.

A great park, well appointed with clean, maintained facilities. A short stroll over the dune to the patrolled beach, and everything else you might need available from the shops that hem the street. Dog friendly too.
Tried calling about twenty times in the last three days, left my name and number several times and I still haven’t heard anything. No happy

Very crowded with kids and dogs. Small, hard to get onto sites with even the worst site jammed against the main road still being charged exorbitant prices. Couldn't believe that a park of this size had only one working dump point. Very noisy at night with extreme noise from road and entertainment areas.  Very rude and unhelpful woman on council phone booking centre.
Great position and very well kept grounds.
Great facilities, perfect location right next to the beach - cafes, coles and dans down the road.

We just had our first visit to this park, and there really isn't anything we could say that we didn't enjoy! Our experience with Coolum Beach Holiday Park was incredible from the get-go.

We have 3 dachshunds (one who is 16 years old and does nothing but sleep!), and our experience with other parks has been that we need to book two sites - with a two dog per site limit. Expecting this to be the case, I called Coolum to book two side by side sites (another park once booked two sites...nowhere near each other...to accommodate the 3 dogs!!), and to our delight once explaining the situation, we were granted approval to keep our three little ones on one site.

When we arrived, we were gob smacked at the size of our site! The space we had (and every site has) was enormous compared to other parks we've stayed in. There was enough room to park our motorhome, extend our awning, and then enough to park our car if we had brought it! So much space is rare in our experience with organised caravan parks! Until now, our go-to for "room to move" has been limited to more rural settings!

The park is so well laid out and has everything we could ever need or want! The facilities were the cleanest and best maintained we've seen to date, and in ample supply! We were just thrilled to not have to walk into gloomy shower blocks or need to keep dozens of coins on hand to bathe or wash our clothes.

The location is amazing! No roads to cross to hit the beach, plenty of things to do and see if you can't keep still (shops, cafe's, parks...you name it!), without needing to drive anywhere.

The people (guests, semi-permanent residents, and most of all the staff) were so friendly. We met plenty of like-minded people for a social chat and found everyone to be friendly and helpful - not always the case in other locations we've visited.

In all for us, Coolum Beach Holiday Park is our new place! We loved every minute, wind and rain throughout our holiday did not even dampen the experience - this was our best camp to date!
Myself and 2 mates stayed on powered sites with his dog stevie and my cockatoo booie, we had a good camp out for 5 nights

Pet friendly area at the northern end.
You have to book early since it's very popular.

Great location and Friendly staff however lots are very tight as is security.

Toilet security doors constantly failing when we were there resulting in access issues.

Door set up also enabled pranksters to effectively lock people in toilet blocks.

Will probably look at others options before returning.
Loved this park,  beachfront access, clean amenities and the guests are a younger generation. Plenty to do nearby
De camping is goed gelegen aan het strand en je hoort de zee. Faciliteiten zijn netjes en alles is goed geregeld. Je loopt zo het stadje in met winkel en terrasjes. Prima verblijf gehad.
Stayed in our own caravan but the facilities were great, location awesome and no issues at all 😃
Fabulous setting on the beach, close to surf club, pubs and coffee shops. Amenities are very clean, grounds are beautifully kept. Great spot for families and pet friendly.
We had a powered site which was good, amenities were great, close to a lot of facilities. Beach was great and at our door front. What more could you ask for.

Wunderbarer, direkt am traumhaften Strand gelegener Camping. Die Toiletten, Duschen und die Campkitchen sind sehr sauber und gepflegt und sämtliche Angestellten helfen, wo Sie nur können. Auch Einkaufsmöglichkeiten und Restaurants sind in wenigen Schritten zu Fuss errwichbar. Unser Aufenthalt war traumhaft!
Excellent, well maintained, and run park
Great set up
Excellent location, clean amenities. Our second time here, we will be back again.
The ranger we dealt with is incredibly rude with absolutely no people skills, felt like he was going out of his way to be rude. Other than that, great location and ammenities.
Would be helpful if they, actually, answered the phone or. failing that, actually rang you back when the recorded message says they will

Good service, good-sized caravan sites.
Should have more toilets for the size of the park.
This is a large, beachside, well appointed and well maintained park. A great location close to shops, yet fairly quiet. It is of the same high standard of all SSHP locations.
Great location. Great beach. Close to shops. Great staff. You won't be disappointed.
Doing too much noisy work in the park during our holidays - it’s not nice!
Great location

A shame, we pay 64 dollars the night to be wake up on the morning by a big noise made by mans who was cutting a tree. The feeling of the unsecurity was big. This one was made by the proximity of the action. We leave in a tent, the camping was aware of this but choose to put us on this place. When we came to complain they didn't want to heard about the situation and said that they can do nothing.
Great holiday park. Friendly staff. Clean sites and amenities. Close to all the shops, pubs, Coles and takeaway food places.

What a great location , nice park well maintained. The staff are awful, we did a group booking many months ago and we were no where near each other. When i nicely asked about if they were very abrunt and told me in a rude manner that just because we did a group booking doesn’t mean we would be put together . An elderly man was in the office at the same time asking for help and they were disrespectful and abrupt to him. Another lovely man who was camped next to us went to the office to ask if they could stay another night and the woman in the reception took him into the office and was going off at him. I know their job is busy but the council needs to review who they’ve got in these jobs. Plenty of nice friendly respectful people out there

I would give no stars if I could. I’m extremely disappointed that I was lied to about not losing a cancellation fee for $50 if I postponed our one night stay. Not only that they booked our night stay at a different holiday park to which I had stated at the beginning and time of payment, therefore a huge mistake on their behalf.I booked originally through the Sunshine Coast Holiday Park website. If I chose to rebook I would have to rebook with an additional night. I feel extremely ripped off and if a company can not admit their mistake rather than blaming the customer I should have received a full refund, they have completely lost any future business with me.  This business needs to do better, especially their customer service.

This place is a great spot for us. The facilities are clean, the park is well looked after. So close to the beach, just 20 metres from our camper trailer, and we were on the beach. Would recommend to all.
After speaking with the local Council and the site manager we have now been offered a choice of 2 new sites. Glad they have responded to the issue.
Great park , friendly helpful staff , immaculate amenities , walk to pub , beach , shops .

Be aware if you book as a group they can and will move you with no warning even if requested to stay together and will not advise you of this. We booked and paid as a group months in advance for an off peak time. Was told via the phone there was a notice not to move our sites as we were a part of a group of 4. Unfortunately when we arrived we were advised they can move sites whenever they please and we were placed a number of blocks away from our group. Most frustratingly there was no one on the sites when we got there. Someone did arrive later and were only staying one extra night although there was various empty spots of the same size available for the duration. Staff were incredibly rude, unfriendly and passive aggressive.

Thank-you for a lovely stay this weekend! We had a site right next to the beach which was perfect. Lovely, clean and quiet campgrounds. Staff were super helpful with a late check in for us. We will be back!
Wir sind den Beachwok gegangen. Angenehme 1,5 Stunden Wanderung im bewaldeten Küstenstreifen.
We were visiting some friends staying at the park. There is a part of the park where you can have your dog stay. Great idea 💡

I am so happy that spent here only one night. I didn’t like it at all. They gave us space  169, toilet and shower without light, it was broken two days ago and nobody informed us, light in our space didn’t work also. Disgusting work of reception. It was 25.10.23. There were plenty of spots closer to another toilets with lights. Internet works bad as well.

Coolum Beach Holiday Park is a fantastic holiday park. The park and the amenities are very clean and well maintained.  The staff are so very kind and helpful.  This park is on my annual list now. Highly recommend. Thank you.
Great location. Love the facilities

Always enjoy coming to stay at this caravan park.

However,  I would highly recommend you bring thongs or slip on shoes, as the grass areas are FULL of bindies!
Great park rite on the beach.

We spent 2 nights in one of the cabins. Great park, friendly service, very clean and tidy. Cabin had everything we needed.
Perfect location right on the beach.
Only downside was that the free Wifi didn't work. Otherwise highly recommend.

Showers and toilets are very clean and the grounds are well maintained.
Living in a goldfish bowl.
Booked in with a caravan parks are tight but they have a tractor to park vans if needed ameneties clean as well as camp kitchen

The only issue we have with this park is that there are too many sites requiring tractor assistance to get on and off and the wait time blows out, not good when you have somewhere you need to be by a certain time!! So far we've been waiting 40 mins this morning for assistance to get off our site and still no tractor in sight!!! Perhaps some changes need to be made or more tractors available!!!
Stayed here in our caravan, lovely spot, but the sites are a bit cramped, but that is a minor complaint. The staff will park your caravan if you are not comfortable reversing in.
Great Dog beach, friendly folk but watch the currents in the water.
These people can't even answer their telephone. Not a great way to run a business.

Great location, pet friendly, however the Council and current management are bleeding this park for all it is worth. The grassed sites look like they have not had a break or any rehabilitation work for a long time.
We witnessed a number of vans being parked nose to tail along the rear fence with a very small gap between them. Park rules state Vans must be reversed onto sites for safety and evacuation reasons. I’m guessing this is to allow larger vans onto small sites as well as keeping the income to a maximum.
Time for council to stop thinking about the dollars and re invest in the park.
Spent there for a week. Good quality with showers and laundry. Also right next to the beach and many useful shops.
Great caravan park, nothing bad to say about it
Large caravan park next to the beach!! They have their own beach access to dog beach off-leash
Best Park location hands down

We love Coolum caravan park! It's dog friendly which is great for our fur-babies. The amenities are spotless and modern. And the best part is the beach access. We were there for 4 days last week and we had the best of both worlds in Winter- the beach warm enough to swim in and the shops/restaurants on the otherside for the cold evenings when you don't feel like cooking!
Great location, the staff were very accommodating and helpful.
Coolum beach,Good choice for a day trip or vacation for surfing and swimming lovers...in .. Queensland, Australia

Top spot because of the location right between the beach and the shopping strip. Excellent for those in motor homes as they could walk everywhere.
Be aware that there are no kids facilities within the park and the sites are small and lack privacy. Some screening or bushes between sites would lift this park a little more. It had a bit of a caravan car-park feel, but no problem if you’re not planning to hang around your van all day.
Always a tight fit into this caravan park but staff are great and it is well located.

We were first time-caravaners, asked for generous location and were put on a corner block. This allowed us to start of our few days on the right foot.  Amenities were fantastic always clean and serviced. Access to a dog walking area and the beach (also dog friendly) was two minutes  away. The staff were very helpful both in the office and around the park, couldn't ask for better.
Awesome place close to beach .Amenity block clean . Close to shops
We had a blast there a few weeks back. So good, we are heading back on our way home. Good facilities, great location.
